title: Orders EDA Report
goal: Build a concise exploratory analysis report for orders data: quality checks, core metrics, one revenue trend, one distribution chart, and clear next steps.
business_context: A product team wants to understand order volume, revenue, commission, delivery fee behavior, and whether the data is clean enough for deeper analysis.

related_portfolio_templates.title: Orders EDA mini report
related_portfolio_templates.what_to_write: Write a short Markdown report with goal, dataset shape, quality checks, core metrics, two chart interpretations, limitations, and next steps.
related_portfolio_templates.chart_or_table: Include a daily revenue line chart, a commission or order_sum distribution chart, and a compact metric table.
related_portfolio_templates.readme_bullet: Built an orders EDA report in pandas/matplotlib, validating data quality and summarizing revenue, commission, and next analytical steps.

milestones.title: Read the dataset context
milestones.description: Review what one row in df_orders.csv means and which columns represent money, users, orders, and dates.
checklist: I can explain order_id, user_id, event_date, order_sum, commission, and delivery_fee.
I know which columns are numeric metrics.
I know which column is the time dimension.

milestones.title: Run basic quality checks
milestones.description: Load the dataset and check shape, missing values, duplicates, numeric ranges, and date range.
milestones.starter_code:
import pandas as pd

orders = pd.read_csv("datasets/df_orders.csv")
print(orders.shape)
print(orders.dtypes)
print(orders.isna().sum())
print("duplicate order_id:", orders["order_id"].duplicated().sum())
print(orders[["order_sum", "commission", "delivery_fee"]].describe())
checklist: I checked whether money fields have suspicious values.
I wrote down at least one data quality limitation.
milestones.portfolio_output: Quality checks table and short interpretation.

milestones.title: Calculate core order metrics
milestones.description: Calculate total orders, unique users, total revenue, total commission, average order value, and delivery fee summary.
dataset_hints: Use order_id for orders and user_id for customers.
milestones.starter_code:
metrics = {
    "orders": orders["order_id"].nunique(),
    "users": orders["user_id"].nunique(),
    "revenue": orders["order_sum"].sum(),
    "commission": orders["commission"].sum(),
    "avg_order": orders["order_sum"].mean(),
}
print(metrics)
checklist: I can explain what each metric means.
milestones.portfolio_output: Core metrics block for the report.

milestones.title: Create two portfolio-ready charts
milestones.description: Create a revenue-over-time line chart and one distribution chart for order_sum or commission.
dataset_hints: df_orders.csv has event_date, order_sum, commission, and delivery_fee.
milestones.starter_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

daily = orders.groupby("event_date", as_index=False)["order_sum"].sum().sort_values("event_date")
ax = daily.plot(x="event_date", y="order_sum", figsize=(10, 4), legend=False)
ax.set_title("Daily order revenue")
ax.set_xlabel("Date")
ax.set_ylabel("Revenue")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

ax = orders["commission"].hist(bins=40)
ax.set_title("Commission distribution")
ax.set_xlabel("Commission")
ax.set_ylabel("Orders")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
checklist: I wrote one insight under each chart.

milestones.title: Write the mini report
milestones.description: Turn the analysis into a short report with goal, data, checks, metrics, charts, insights, limitations, and next steps.
dataset_hints: Use the Portfolio Output Guide template.
checklist: The report has a clear goal.
The report has at least three findings.

deliverables: Notebook or script with reproducible EDA
portfolio_prompt: Write a portfolio case study explaining what the orders data shows about revenue, commission, data quality, and what you would investigate next.
